trade.summary: Bilateral trade is significant given Taiwan's semiconductor dominance. France exports aircraft, luxury goods, and chemicals to Taiwan. Taiwan exports semiconductors, electronics, and ICT products to France. TSMC's European expansion (including in Germany) has elevated Taiwan's strategic importance to European supply chain planning.

diplomatic.summary: France maintains official relations with the People's Republic of China and thus has no formal diplomatic relations with Taiwan. However, France maintains unofficial ties through trade offices and parliamentary exchanges. French parliamentarians have visited Taiwan, increasing Beijing's displeasure. France's semiconductor dependency on Taiwan has elevated Taiwan's strategic importance to French policymakers.
recent_incidents: French parliamentary visits to Taiwan (2022-2024) angering Beijing

military.summary: No formal military ties given France's one-China policy. France has occasionally made statements supporting peace and stability in the Taiwan Strait. French naval vessels transit the Taiwan Strait on freedom of navigation operations. France is cautious not to appear to be arming Taiwan.

financial.summary: Growing financial ties driven by Taiwan's semiconductor and tech ecosystem. French companies invest in Taiwan's tech sector. Taiwanese companies (Foxconn, TSMC supply chain) have European operations. France's interest in diversifying semiconductor supply chains has elevated Taiwan commercially.

energy.summary: Very limited direct energy trade. Some cooperation in offshore wind technology (Taiwan has ambitious offshore wind targets). French energy companies have bid on Taiwanese offshore wind projects.

scientific.summary: Meaningful scientific cooperation in semiconductors, AI, and materials science through researcher exchanges and industry collaboration. CNRS has informal research links with Taiwanese institutions. The semiconductor supply chain creates deep technical cooperation.
